title: Intro to Judaism Unit 2
description:
Judaism is built around community, ritual, tradition, and prayer. If you are Jewish, interested in converting or raising a Jewish family and looking to learn more, delve deeper, and connect — learn with us!
Our engaging, university-accredited Intro To Judaism course is interactive and encourages questions, discussion, and hands-on learning. No knowledge of Hebrew is required. This Introduction to Judaism class will be taught in three units. For those who are on the path for conversion, please register for the full course — all three units, as this is not a drop-in model.
Click here for schedule and registration!
Looking to join our next cohort in the fall? Contact Hilary at helfman@bethsholomsf.org for more information

title: Alef Bet & Beyond!
description:
Taught by Rabbi SAM Luckey

Weekly on Thursday evenings from 7:30 to 8:45 pm ONLINE for 5 sessions
Come learn to decode the alef-bet! In this five week course, we will learn the Hebrew letters and vowels using fun visual and aural mnemonics. Home practice between classes is highly encouraged.
Whether this is your first encounter with the alef-bet or a refresher, we will build a foundation for reading sacred Hebrew (and Aramaic) texts such as Torah, Talmud, and liturgy. This class will prepare you to join a trope class, a 700 Benches Beit Midrash (house of study) class, or other workshops that involve studying Hebrew or Aramaic texts in their original languages.
Dates:
February 26
March 5, 12, 19, 26
Cost:*

Members: $100 for series
Non-members: $125 for series

→ Plus a workbook that costs $6-15, ordered separately
Note: You will need to get a copy of the book L’shon Ha-Kodesh. You can find used copies on the internet for $6-15.

*No one turned away for lack of ability to pay; please pay what you can at registration or email Rabbi SAM if you need a full discount.

REGISTER HERE
Please email Rabbi SAM Luckey at rabbisam@kehillasynagogue.org if you have any questions.

description:

title: Volunteer Opportunity - In-Person: Sacred Land Committee Hands On Gardening
description:
Avodah/Volunteer hours opportunity:
The Sacred Land Committee is dedicated to honoring the heritage of the Lisjan Ohlone people and our own Jewish heritage. We are seeking to bring the unceded land (called Huchiun) of the Lisjan Ohlone people, on which Kehilla rests, into harmony with Indigenous and Jewish values. We will be refurbishing the outside spaces (like the courtyard, and front and back gardens) and reconnecting with the land and its spirit. If you enjoy gardening with a vision of environmental  integrity you are encouraged to join us.
For more information, or to let us know you’re coming, please contact us at: infoslc@kehillasynagogue.org

title: Identity and its Discontents: An Evening of Culture and Conversation with Rabbi Kendell Pinkney
location: Congregation Rodef Sholom, Congregation Rodef Sholom, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description: To live as a Black Jew in North America is to live with a keen awareness of just how precarious group belonging can be, yet a felt sense of ‘belonging’ is a core human need. Between this self-evident need for community and the complexities of contemporary identity, lies a unique space bounded by subtle challenges and creative possibilities. Join theatre artist and rabbi, Kendell Pinkney, for an evening of conversation, experimentation, and excerpts from his theatre work that probes the boundaries of Black, Jewish, and rabbinic identity. The evening will include havdalah.

title: “Identity and its Discontents”
location: Congregation Rodef Sholom, 170 N. San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, United States
description: New York-based playwright Rabbi Kendell Pinkney discusses culture and community as a Black Jew.

title: Havdallah at the Observatory
location: Chabot Space and Science Center, 10000 Skyline Blvd, Oakland, California, 94619
description:
Registration for this event is free. Donations are welcome.
Say Goodby to Shabbat and Hello to the Night
Join Wilderness Torah at the Chabot Space and Science Center for an evening of ritual, wonder, and connection to the living cosmos.
RegisterEvent DetailsGather with us as the sun sinks into the Bay and darkness gathers. Rooted in earth-based Jewish tradition, we will mark the moment when three stars appear with havdallah, saying goodbye to Shabbat and hello to the night. From there, we turn our bodies and our blessings upward, blessing the month’s new moon with a Kiddush Levana ritual.
Join Wilderness Torah at the Chabot Space and Science Center for an evening of ritual, wonder, and connection to the living cosmos. After the blessings, there will be time to gaze into the heavens through Chabot’s three observatory telescopes, experiencing the night sky as our ancestors once did, with awe, humility, and gratitude.

title: Avodat Lev Hike - Palo Alto
location: Wunderlich County Park
description:
Registration is free. Donations are welcome.
Let’s Get Together, Outside!
We’re heading into the hills for a hike that’s part adventure, part heart-opening, part “wow, look at that view.” Avodat Lev means service of the heart, and this walk is an invitation to slow down, breathe deeper, and notice what’s alive around and within us.
Expect good company, open sky, winding trails, and moments of reflection tucked between footsteps. You can come solo, with a friend, with a partner, with kids, with anyone who loves being outside.
Just bring your walking shoes and a little curiosity.
RegisterEvent Details

description:

title: History is Calling! Celebrating 130 Years  of Jewish History in the Bay Area
location: Magnes Collection of Jewish Life and Art, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA, 94720, United States
description:
Presented by J. The Jewish News of Northern California and the Magnes Collection of Jewish Art and Life.
Join the J. and the Magnes for a free, family-friendly afternoon of programs and activities celebrating 130 Years of Jewish History in the Bay Area.

Be a part of our audio history project: Record a voice mail sharing your Jewish history story.
Hear from local archivists Susan Morris and Lara Michels as they look back at Jewish identity and culture in the Bay Area
Dive into Bay Area Jewish history with a hands-on, family-friendly art workshop
Discover local Jewish material culture and journalism from the Magnes collection and the J’s archive.
Connect with the community during a reception with light refreshments

Sunday, March 22, 2026 | 1:00-4:00 pm
at the Magnes Collection of Jewish Art and Life, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA
Activities and Reception begin at 1:00pm
Historian Panel begins at 2:30pm
RSVP

For questions about accessibility or require accommodations to participate in this event, please reach out to magnes@berkeley.edu or (510) 643-2526 with as much advance notice as possible.

About the Panelists
Lara Michels is an archivist and historian. She serves as Head of Archival Processing at The Bancroft Library, where she oversees work on all of Bancroft’s archival collections, including those of the Magnes’ former Western Jewish History Center. She has also been an archivist at the Pacific Gas & Electric Company and at the Judah L. Magnes Museum.
Susan Morris is a a former archivist and curator in the Western Jewish History Center, Judah Magnes Museum, Berkeley. She served as Executive Director of the Judah Magnes Museum following the retirement of Seymour Fromer, Magnes museum co-founder. Susan is the author of A Traveler’s Guide to Pioneer Jewish Cemeteries of the California Gold Rush. She is a Rosston Society member of the UC Berkeley University Library.

title: A Taste of Judaism (a free 3-part series)
location: Temple Israel of Alameda, 3183 Mecartney Road, Alameda, 94502, United States
description:
Alameda– Temple Israel of Alameda announced the return of the 3-session program called A Taste of Judaism® to be held over three Sunday afternoons, March 15, 22, and 29, from 3:00 – 4:30 pm, at Temple Israel. The series will be led by Jason Harris–Temple Israel member, local Jewish educator, and creator of the Jew Oughta Know Jewish history podcast–is all about introducing you to what is delicious about Judaism: sacred stories, beliefs and values, and diverse community – with plenty of time for your questions. (We love questions!)
Designed for “beginners” from any religious background (or none), no previous knowledge of Judaism is assumed or required. All are welcome: interfaith couples, individuals exploring conversion, people with Jewish heritage who are “out of practice,” non-Jewish in-laws or grandparents, and anyone who wants to know a bit more about Judaism in a friendly, safe environment.
Whatever has sparked your curiosity to learn more, A Taste of Judaism® is a great place to start learning.
Support for A Taste of Judaism® is generously provided by the National Center to Encourage Judaism.

description:

The book is available for purchase here. Each gathering is a drop in space – feel free to read just the chapters that are being discussed and join in even if you aren’t reading the whole book sequentially. Join here.
*z”l stands for zichrono livracha, May his memory be a blessing.

title: A dialogue of voice and piano
location: Albert and Janet Schultz Cultural Arts Hall (Bldg F), 3921 Fabian Way, Palo Alto, 94303, United States
description:
Svetlana Portnyanskaya
A Dialogue of Voice and Piano
For the first time — in a duo with renowned jazz pianist and composer Sergey Chipenko
Join us for a unique concert where the power of the human voice meets the artistry of jazz piano.
Singer Svetlana Portnyanskaya and acclaimed pianist and composer Sergey Chipenko come together in a dynamic duo performance that moves effortlessly between jazz, world classics, and contemporary music.
The program spans a wide musical range — from refined jazz pieces and timeless international songs to vibrant Latin rhythms and modern Hebrew and Yiddish music, presented in original arrangements.
This is a concert built on live interaction, musical freedom, and genuine emotion, created in the moment and shared with the audience.

Every year, Jewish tradition asks us to count forty-nine days — one by one — from Pesach to Shavuot, with no reason given. Join us as we explore the origins and evolving rituals of Sefirat HaOmer, and pick up practical materials to make your daily count more meaningful this year.

title: Energy Audits as a Starting Point
location: Virtual, Zoom / Online
description:
12pm ET/9am PT

Wondering where to start with climate action?
In this webinar, we’ll demystify what an energy audit actually is, help guide you to the appropriate type of audit for your organization.
RegisterEvent DetailsAn energy audit is one of the smartest first steps, and it generally pays for itself. An audit shows you exactly where your building is losing energy and money, then gives you a clear roadmap for what to do about it. The best part? Many audits are available for free through state and utility programs, and most recommendations are low-cost or no-cost changes that save you money right away.
In this webinar, we’ll demystify what an energy audit actually is, help guide you to the appropriate type of audit for your organization, and introduce a special group discount opportunity through Energy for Purpose. Energy audits take the guesswork out of climate action while saving you money.
This program is produced as a part of the Jewish Climate Leadership Coalition, learn more here.
With enough interest from Jewish organizations, Adamah may be able to facilitate a group discount on professional energy audits from our partner, Energy for Purpose. If your organization is interested in getting a discounted energy audit, fill out this form to let us know

title: Hands-On Passover Cooking Workshop
location: CA
description:
Hands-On Passover Cooking Workshop with Aliza Grayevsky Somekh of Bishulim SF
Early-bird registration by 3/16 $150 per person; after 3/17 $180 per person | Registration required | Limited to 12 adults
Join Chef Aliza for an intimate, hands-on Passover cooking workshop that explores the rich ritual and cultural diversity of the Seder table. Together, we’ll prepare a festive Passover meal inspired by Jewish communities from around the world, learning traditional dishes and creative contemporary interpretations along the way.
Using the symbols of the Seder plate as our inspiration, Aliza will weave tradition into modern recipes for the holiday meal. Participants will cook the dishes and prepare several versions of charoset from different Jewish traditions and communities, while discovering new spices, flavors, and stories from across the Jewish world.
We’ll cook side by side, then sit down together to enjoy the meal we’ve prepared, share stories of the holiday, and connect around the table.
The event will take place at a private house in the El Cerrito Hills. Directions will be provided after tickets are purchased.
Workshop is kosher-style meat format meaning there will be no mixing of meat and dairy. The meat used will be meat that is permitted to eat, but it will not have a kosher certification.
PURCHASE TICKETS HERE
About Aliza
Aliza Grayevsky Somekh, an Israeli educator and chef. She is an eighth-generation Jerusalemite. Aliza specializes in Israeli culture. After a long career in informal education and Israeli culture working with U.S and Israel-based Jewish organizations In 2016, she opened Bishulim SF, a boutique catering company fusing California ingredients with Israeli flavors. Drawing from her roots and culinary expertise, she creates food experiences that highlight Israel’s diverse heritage and tell meaningful stories, building connections through cuisine.
Website: bishulimsf.com
Instagram: instagram.com/bishulim_sf

title: The Torah of Place and Diaspora
description:
Register Here
Taught by: Rabbi Dev Noily
Weekly on Mondays from 7-9 pm for 6 sessions, in person only, starting on February 16th.
Description:
What does the Torah teach about our relationship to land and about how we are to live with integrity and in harmony with the land? What’s the role of the land of Israel/Palestine in the covenantal relationship between the Jewish people and YHVH / G-d? How does Torah and later Jewish teaching guide us in our experience as Jews living in, and embracing diaspora?
Together we will study both sacred Jewish texts and current scholarship that is (re-)revealing Jewish diaspora and exile as generative, creative and necessary – both physically and spiritually – for Jewish thriving. There will be a modest amount of reading to do between sessions.
Please note: This class is not a drop-in course. Students must register for the full series. Please arrive early. We will start welcoming people 30 minutes before class and will conclude 30 minutes after class begins.
Dates:

Mon., February 23

title: Game On! Mexican Train, Rummikub & Scrabble
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join others for a delightful afternoon of fun and games – literally! Choose from one of three games. Learn and play!

Mexican Train has become a popular game with dominos in which the goal is for participants to play all of the tiles from their hands onto one or more chains – or trains – emanating from a central hub or “station.”

Rummikub is a tile-based game for 2 or more players combining elements of the card game rummy and mahjong.

Scrabble is a popular word game for 2-4 players where participants use letter tiles to create words on a board, aiming to connect them to existing words (similar to a crossword puzzle).

Players of all levels are welcome!

Space is limited. Advance registration required.

The Art of Havruta: A Mishnah Study Series with Hadar
Join our SF Beit Midrash for three months of learning through a blend of independent havruta-learning and three in-person Beit Midrash sessions, open to everyone.
At each Beit Midrash session, Rabbi Kasher will offer brief teachings on the philosophy of havruta study and guide us through the themes and structure of the Mishnah chapters from Hadar’s Project Zug Course “The Mishnah In Context”. title: Moving Climate Action Forward: A Two-Part Intensive
description:
Location will be emailed to registrants prior to program date.
Session 1: March 25, Wednesday at 12pm ET/9am PT
Session 2: April 15, Wednesday at 12pm ET/9am PT

Climate action inside a Jewish organization isn’t always a technical challenge, it’s also a leadership one.
This two-part intensive is designed to give you practical skills and peer support to lead climate action in your organization with more confidence and clarity.
RegisterEvent DetailsWhether you’re just getting started or you’ve already mapped out a plan, the hardest part is often the same: building momentum, navigating competing priorities, and bringing others along with you.
Our new two-part intensive, Moving Climate Action Forward, is designed to give you the practical skills to build momentum, engage stakeholders, and keep making progress. You’ll leave with a stronger sense of how to approach your work, how to engage stakeholders, and how to keep moving forward when things get hard.
Sessions are interactive and grounded in real challenges. Come ready to think, share, and learn alongside peers from across the Jewish community. This is a cohort style program with limited space, register to claim your spot!

Session 1:
Building your approach to climate action: how to think about where you are, where you want to go, and how to make progress.
Session 2:
Navigating the people side: stakeholder buy-in, common pitfalls, and how to stay on track when momentum stalls.

Note: This program is open to members of the Jewish Climate Leadership Coalition. Membership is free! Learn more at www.adamah.org/coalition or email us at coalition@adamah.org.

title: Excavating the BeShT Taught by Rabbi Diane Elliot
description:
Bi-weekly on Wednesdays from 5:00 to 6:30 pm Pacific Time, ONLINE for 8 sessions
Hosted by the Aquarian Minyan Yeshiva and co-sponsored by Kehilla and Havurat Shir Ha-Yam.
Have you ever wondered how the spiritual teachings of the Jewish Renewal movement are rooted in Jewish tradition? Have you acquired Rabbi Burt Jacobson’s book Living in the Presence, A Personal Quest for the Baal Shem Tov, but found its length and density daunting?
Join Rabbi Diane Elliot for this series designed to unpack some of the essential teachings of the Baal Shem Tov (also known as the BeShT), the great 18th century shaman, kabbalist and mystic who sparked a revival of Jewish spirituality across Eastern Europe that echoes through the Renewal Judaism of today. Using Rabbi Burt’s book, along with primary source texts, we’ll explore the context in which the BeShT and his teachings arose, the kabbalistic roots of early Hasidism, and some of Hasidism’s most essential innovations––an-opened hearted, joyful Jewishness, steeped in love of God and our fellow beings, supported by holy teachers or tzaddikim.
Each class will include niggun (song), textual learning, contemplative practice, and conversation. No experience needed; everyone is welcome!

Rabbi Diane Elliot (she/her) is a teacher, ritual leader, and spiritual director based in the Bay Area who inspires her students and directees to become clearer channels for Presence through awareness and movement practices, chant, and nuanced interpretations of Jewish sacred text. She came to the rabbinate some 25 years ago, after a career as a dance performer and somatic movement therapist. Rabbi Diane directs ALEPH’s Embodying Spirit programs, serves on the Advisory Council of the Shalom Center, is a founding steward of the Taproot Community, and has authored four books of sacred poetry, most recently Traces. Learn more about her work at www.whollypresent.org.

Rabbi Burt Jacobson z”l was ordained at the Jewish Theological Seminary in 1966 and founded Kehilla Community Synagogue in Piedmont, California in 1983. An early leader in Jewish Renewal, Rabbi Burt worked for nuclear disarmament in the 1970’s and was an early proponent of two states in Israel/Palestine. He was one of the first rabbis to train as a spiritual director and served in that capacity for some 30 years. In addition to Living in the Presence (Monkfish, 2024), he self-published This Precious Moment, The Wisdom of the Baal Shem Tov (2016), and his egalitarian liturgical translations and poems can be found in the liturgies of multiple denominations. Rabbi Burt and Rabbi Diane were together as loving life partners from 2003 until his death from cancer in 2024.

Excavating the BeShT Syllabus
January 7  Grounding the BeShT in History
January 21  Kabbalist Roots of Hasidism
February 4  The Role of the Tzaddik (Rebbe)
February 18  Joy as Spiritual Practice
March 11  Love and Yearning
March 25  Sweetening Suffering
April 8  Receiving the One
April 29 Living in the Presence

Dates:
January 7, 21
February 4, 18
March 11, 25
April 8, 29

Join us for our annual fundraiser in celebration of the stories we carry forward — the memories, traditions, and moments that connect us across generations. title: REAL presents Smiley Poswolsky on Friendship in the Age of Loneliness
location: Congregation Rodef Sholom, Congregation Rodef Sholom, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
How do we create and maintain real human connection in an age of AI, digital overwhelm, and social fragmentation?
While we are more digitally connected than ever, many people report feeling increasingly disconnected, finding that forming and sustaining meaningful friendships as an adult is surprisingly difficult. Join workplace belonging expert and keynote speaker Adam “Smiley” Poswolsky as he explores his recent book, Friendship in the Age of Loneliness, and invites us to rethink friendship as an essential pillar of a well-lived life.
In this inspiring and optimistic talk, Smiley will share:
*Practical habits and accessible ways to build meaningful connections in a fast-paced world.
*Strategies for making new friends and deepening existing relationships beyond work and family roles.
*How small, purposeful acts can counter isolation and help us show up for one another more fully.
Join us to rediscover how to prioritize the human ties that matter most. A reception and book signing will follow the event.
This program is part of REAL Mental Health Initiative’s Spring Speakers Series.
Rodef Sholom’s REAL Mental Health Initiative is generously supported by the Laszlo N. Tauber Family Foundation.

title: “Friendship in the Age of Loneliness”

title: Alef Bet & Beyond!
description:
Taught by Rabbi SAM Luckey

Weekly on Thursday evenings from 7:30 to 8:45 pm ONLINE for 5 sessions
Come learn to decode the alef-bet! In this five week course, we will learn the Hebrew letters and vowels using fun visual and aural mnemonics. Home practice between classes is highly encouraged.
Whether this is your first encounter with the alef-bet or a refresher, we will build a foundation for reading sacred Hebrew (and Aramaic) texts such as Torah, Talmud, and liturgy. This class will prepare you to join a trope class, a 700 Benches Beit Midrash (house of study) class, or other workshops that involve studying Hebrew or Aramaic texts in their original languages.

title: ONLINE ONLY: Shabbat Morning - Tzav - NEW TIME -
description:
Shabbat Morning – Tzav
The service is starting earlier [9:45] than most services to allow people time to get to No Kings Day protests.
Join us for a celebratory online only Shabbat morning service with Rabbi David Cooper, and Beth Dickinson, exploring parashat Tzav. In Leviticus 7:11-15 the Torah describes a sacrifice called Shelamim sometimes translated as the “well-being” sacrifice but it may be better understood as the “make-whole” offering. We’ll consider how that may apply this day as we prepare to protest how this administration is a source of brokenness in so many ways.
JOIN US HERE
Kehilla requires masking at all in-person, indoor events, except youth B’mitzvah ceremonies (mask-optional b’mitzvah ceremonies will be noted as such in the event title). See the full Kehilla COVID-19 Safety Protocols here, and see other accessibility information here.
All events at Kehilla are scent-free. Click here for information about what it means to be scent-free.

title: Hiking at Shoreline Lake Park and Dinner
description:
Bay Area Jewish Singles Havurah
Please join us for an early evening hike at **Shoreline Lake Park.**
**Shoreline** is a vibrant oasis of preservation, recreation and entertainment, the 750-acre park is home to over 30 protected species and provides a wide range of recreation opportunities including: a robust trail system, a 50-acre sailing lake, an 18-hole championship golf course, a dog park, a kite flying area, as well as athletic fields, historic Rengstorff House and two restaurants.

**Itinerary & Trail info:** We will walk around the lake and then take another trail along the Bay. Total time there and back is 1.5 hours.

**Where to park and meet: Parking is free but please arrive early to find parking.** Park in the parking lot by the boathouse. If there is no parking in that lot, please turn around and park at the parking lot at Michael’s Restaurant and then just walk to the boathouse.

**Details:**
· The event will be rescheduled for another day in case of moderate to heavy rain.

* Please bring water, sunscreen, and layered clothing.
* The bathroom is located in front of the boat rental
* If you are running late, please walk along the lake and you will find us.

**Optional Dinner: After the hike in Downtown Mountain View on Castro Street**

Feel free to invite your friends!

See you there!

title: The Torah of Place and Diaspora
description:
Register Here
Taught by: Rabbi Dev Noily
Weekly on Mondays from 7-9 pm for 6 sessions, in person only, starting on February 16th.
Description:
What does the Torah teach about our relationship to land and about how we are to live with integrity and in harmony with the land? What’s the role of the land of Israel/Palestine in the covenantal relationship between the Jewish people and YHVH / G-d? How does Torah and later Jewish teaching guide us in our experience as Jews living in, and embracing diaspora?
Together we will study both sacred Jewish texts and current scholarship that is (re-)revealing Jewish diaspora and exile as generative, creative and necessary – both physically and spiritually – for Jewish thriving. There will be a modest amount of reading to do between sessions.
Please note: This class is not a drop-in course. Students must register for the full series. Please arrive early. We will start welcoming people 30 minutes before class and will conclude 30 minutes after class begins.
Dates:

Mon., February 16
Mon., February 23

((no class on March 2nd))

Mon., March 9

title: Game On! Mexican Train, Rummikub & Scrabble
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join others for a delightful afternoon of fun and games – literally! Choose from one of three games. Learn and play!

Mexican Train has become a popular game with dominos in which the goal is for participants to play all of the tiles from their hands onto one or more chains – or trains – emanating from a central hub or “station.”

Rummikub is a tile-based game for 2 or more players combining elements of the card game rummy and mahjong.

Scrabble is a popular word game for 2-4 players where participants use letter tiles to create words on a board, aiming to connect them to existing words (similar to a crossword puzzle).

Players of all levels are welcome!

Space is limited. Advance registration required.

description:

We’ll have popcorn, Dr Brown’s soda, and plenty of movie snacks. (And matzah if you’re feeling it). Join us as we carry this tradition forward a 4th year! Much like the Israelites when they crossed the Red Sea, this event is free.

title: Chabad S. Clara - Passover Seder! (100+ attending)
description:
Mountain Jew
🔒 Want to attend? This Meetup won't show location or tickets — full access is in the "About Mountain Jew" section below.
Celebrate Passover in the warmth of Community! Relive the Jewish redemption from slavery & enjoy:

* A Delicious Passover Dinner
* Argentinian Brisket & Vegetarian Options
* A Participatory Retelling of the Story
* International Selection of Kosher Wines
* Tasting the special Passover Delicacies of Haroset & Marror
* Hand-Baked “Shmurah” Matzah from Israel
EARLY BIRD Adult $45 \| Standard $54 \|
——————————————————
✡️ About Mountain Jew
Mountain Jew is a community exclusively for Jewish young professionals in the Bay Area. Jewish people make up less than 0.2% of the world's population, so having spaces like this to connect with one another is incredibly meaningful — we kindly ask that this be respected.
This is where people don't just show up to events — they actually meet, connect, and build real friendships. From hikes to dinners to social gatherings, it's a community that's active, welcoming, and easy to plug into.For the comfort and safety of our members, full event details (including exact locations) are shared only with verified MJ members.
➡️ Join us: https://mountainjew.org
Get verified to access the full calendar, RSVP to events, and join the WhatsApp community.

title: Shabbat Dinner
description:
Bay Area Jewish Singles Havurah
Please join us for our Shabbat dinner- Passover themed on April 3, 2026. This will be held in San Jose, at the home of one of our group members. The address will be shared closer to the date of the event.

Attendance is limited to 20 individuals.

You're welcome to invite your single Jewish friends. Nevertheless, we request that your guests register for the event on the meetup site directly. Thus, the guest feature is disabled for this event.

We are asking for a donation of $3 per person to offset the cost of the MeetUp fees ( to be paid every 6 months).

This will be a potluck dinner- Passover themed as it will be the third night of Passover. We will NOT be having the formal Passover dinner nor reading the Haggadah, just getting together to socialize.

Food: If possible, please bring foods that are typically served on Passover, and please bring a dish large enough to serve 5-7 people. You are welcome to bring a bottle of wine as well, if you would like. Please no pork, bacon, shellfish (shrimp, clams etc.) nor bread products.

We will provide plates, cups, cutlery and non-alcoholic drinks. The stove, microwave and oven will be available for you to reheat your dish. Nevertheless, due to time constraints, please bring the dish already prepared. Please write what you will bring in the comments section so that others can see.

title: Shabbat Services
location: Sanctuary, 327 MacArthur Blvd, Oakland, CA, 94610, United States
description:
Our Shabbat service is available in our sanctuary and online at https://TBAoakland.org/stream.
Our Shabbat service is both Conservative and egalitarian. The siddur (prayerbook) we use, Shirat Avraham or The Song of Abraham, was written by TBA’s Rabbi Mark Bloom as “a siddur dedicated to those who wish to increase the meaning and accessibility in traditional Jewish prayer.”
We invite all to join in a Shabbat Kiddush following the service.

title: Dinner and movie
description:
Bay Area Jewish Singles Havurah
5:30- 7:00- Optional Dinner- I will post the restaurant location at a later time. As it is Passover- we may be limited in food options- restaurants with alternatives to bread options.

7:30 PM - 9:30 PM- Movie "Oxygen"

Anat, a single mother and enthusiastic teacher of literature at a primary school, can't wait for her son to be discharged from the army. But right on the day of his planned return, war breaks out on the border with Lebanon.

The condition of her traumatized father worsens, and from her son comes the news that all discharges have been suspended. Anat sets heaven and earth in motion to get her son home — but when he does return briefly, their close relationship is put under enormous pressure.
**Cast:** Dana Ivgy, Ben Sultan, Marek Rozenbaum
**Director**: Netalie Braun
95 min \ 2025 \ Hebrew with English subtitles
**[Buy tickets](https://www.tickettailor.com/events/oshmanfamilyjcc/2087924)**[ ](https://www.tickettailor.com/events/oshmanfamilyjcc/2087924)

title: Midweek Musical Matinee: "Klezmer and Friends"
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Come join us for a live, in-person concert filled with beautiful music and interesting insights.

Klezmer, in its essence, has always been about joy for its own sake.
In honor of the Passover holiday, join accordionist Dan Cantrell, visiting from Los Angeles, and clarinetist Asaf Ophir, for a delightful matinee devoted exactly to that — Klezmer and joy!

These dynamic artitsts will play some classics, some favorites, some oldies, some newbies, and some more exotic tunes that just go so well together that they couldn’t pass them up.

Mixed in with interesting storytelling, this is going to be a most memorable event!

title: Shabbat Shmooze
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join us in the JCC Lobby for a very special Shabbat Shmooze! As Passover ends, we’ll celebrate Mimouna– the Maghrebi Jewish feast marking the return to eating hametz (leavened bread), which is forbidden during Passover. Featuring Sephardic-Mizrachi songs by Rachel Valfer of the Qadim Ensemble, along with traditional delicacies to taste! While you’re here, be sure to walk through our galleries to see She’erit, the artwork of Chama Metchaly, before this exhibit leaves.

About this exhibit:
She’erit, שְׁאֵרִית, is the Hebrew word for “remnant.” It evokes what survives erasure, what endures exile, what persists across generations despite the forces of assimilation and antisemitism. Rooted in the intersecting spiritual and cultural lineages of Amazigh and Sephardi heritage, the exhibition transforms devastation into art, not to beautify pain, but to dignify indigenous inheritance against the erasures of colonial Islamist ideology. This is a reclamation of narrative, of space, of voice, and breath.

We will gather every 2nd Friday, monthly.

Free event! All are welcome. No registration, just drop-in!

title: Cultural Xperiences: "Magical Morocco"
location: Grand Hôtel Villa de France, Rue d'Angleterre, Tanger, Tanger-Tétouan-Al Hoceïma, Morocco
description:
From seaside to mountain-top to desert, from historical sites to modern wonders, from natural to man-made beauty, with a rich history of Berbers, Arabs, Christians & Jews. Morocco has it all!

Travel on this adventure — created specifically for GenX’ers! — to this marvelous, magical North African country, so different from most Arab-dominant countries, Morocco has its own unique, fascinating culture – a blend of indigenous Berber, Arabian, Sub-Saharan African, and European influences. In fact, ethnically speaking, though Morocco is composed mainly of Berbers and Arabs, or a mix of the two, some segments of the population are descendants of refugees from Spain and Portugal who fled during the Inquisition to be welcomed by the Moroccans.

Explore bustling imperial cities (Fes, Meknes & Marrakech). Experience ocean-side towns (Tangier, Essaouira). Stay in a charming hillside retreat (Chefchaouen) and in the mountain region (Dades). Camp (if you like) in among the dunes at the edge of the Sahara Desert (Merzouga). Enjoy “off the tourist track” locals (Taroudant). Even stop in a nomadic home for a cup of tea.

Visit several UNESCO World Heritage sites including the Archaeological site of Volubilis, the historic city of Meknes, the Ksar of Ait-Ben-Haddou, the old Medinas of Essaouira, Fes and Marrakesh.

Marvel at the natural beauty of the Atlas mountains, the majesty of the Todgha Gorge, the serenity of the graceful sand dunes at the edge of the desert.Discover the traditions of hand-made beauty – stunning handicrafts still being made with pride! Meet craftsman engaged in ceramics, mosaics, metalworking, fabric weaving, rug making, wood working and more!Learn about indigenous products such as rose oil, supplied to the French perfume industry; Argan oil – the wonder of the beauty & cosmetic industries; Saffron – the world’s most expensive spice, and more!

With a rich itinerary of traditional and “off the beaten path” sites and experiences, this exciting journey is not to be missed.

For details & pricing, contact James at jsokol@marinjcc.org or register for a “ticket” via this Eventbrite registration.

Cultural Xperiences offered by the National JCC Adult & Senior Alliance & the Osher Marin JCC are open to all.
JCC Members and non-members; people of all backgrounds and faiths are invited to join in these enriching & delicious cultural programs!

title: Yom HaShoa: "Forever Remembered" with SF Holocaust Center
description:
Forever Remembered – An Immersive Sound Installation for Yom HaShoah
Date: Monday, April 13, 2026
Time: 12:00 p.m. – 6:00 p.m. (Sound Installation & Hall of Remembrance)
Location: JCC San Francisco, 3200 California St, San Francisco
Cost: Free; Registration encouraged
Description: Join the JFCS Holocaust Center and our community partners for Forever Remembered, a powerful new community memorial experience honoring the six million Jewish lives lost during the Holocaust.
Building on the sacred tradition of the "Reading of Names," this year’s commemoration features an immersive sound installation by Bay Area artist Forest Reid. Throughout the afternoon, the Hall of Remembrance will be transformed into a space for deep listening and reflection. Visitors will encounter a continuous soundscape of community voices—neighbors, descendants, and students—reading the names of victims from our local archives, weaving together a collective act of remembrance.
The Experience Includes:

A Living Soundscape: Listen to the names of family and community members read by the voices of today.

Space for Reflection: Opportunities for candle-lighting and personal meditation.

Memorial Books: Engage with Yizkor (memorial) books containing the names of those honored in the project.
Add Your Voice: Forever Remembered is a living memorial that grows through your participation. We invite you to record yourself reading names from the Holocaust Center’s archives or your own family history to be included in the installation.
Register to Attend or Submit a Voice Recording
Presented by the JFCS Holocaust Center in partnership with the JCCSF, Congregation Beth Sholom, Congregation Emanu-El, Congregation Sha’ar Zahav, and Congregation Sherith Israel.

title: Game On! Mexican Train, Rummikub & Scrabble
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join others for a delightful afternoon of fun and games – literally! Choose from one of three games. Learn and play!

Mexican Train has become a popular game with dominos in which the goal is for participants to play all of the tiles from their hands onto one or more chains – or trains – emanating from a central hub or “station.”

Rummikub is a tile-based game for 2 or more players combining elements of the card game rummy and mahjong.

Scrabble is a popular word game for 2-4 players where participants use letter tiles to create words on a board, aiming to connect them to existing words (similar to a crossword puzzle).

Players of all levels are welcome!

Space is limited. Advance registration required.

title: Sofrut (Hebrew calligraphy) Workshop: 6 Part Series
location: Chochmat Halev, 2215 Prince St, Berkeley, 94705, United States
description:
Exact location TBD

Learn Hebrew Caligraphy
Join us for a six-part Sofrut ‘Hebrew Calligraphy’ series, exploring the ancient ancestral skill of creating feather quills, oak galls, and the living language of Hebrew letters.

Register TodayEvent DetailsLong before we were ever called the People of the Book, we were a people of hands, ink, and parchment. Writing Hebrew was an ancestral craft, rooted in the living world, shaped by feather quills, oak galls, and patient attention.
Join us for a six-part Sofrut, ‘Hebrew Calligraphy’ series, exploring these ancient skills. Together, we will learn how to make oak gall ink from the land, cut our own feather quills, and practice the sacred art of forming Hebrew letters by hand. This is a slow, embodied invitation into relationship with Hebrew as a living language and a sacred form.
This series is perfect for those seeking to deepen their connection to Hebrew and Jewish tradition, as well as for those encountering the Aleph Bet for the very first time. No prior experience is necessary, only curiosity, care, and a willingness to learn with your hands.

title: No Lines in the Sea: featuring Elana Sasson
location: Magnes Collection of Jewish Life and Art, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA, 94720, United States
description:
A FLOWING THROUGH TIME AND TRADITION PROGRAM
Don’t miss the chance to hear the dynamic, internationally acclaimed musician and recording artist Elana Sasson, in concert with Queralt Giralt on cello and Bahar Badiei on oud.
Ten songs about water, sung in seven languages: Spanish, Ladino, Greek, Persian, Gilaki, Kurdish, and Catalan. Water as hope and survival, as celebration and mourning, as memory and loss. Drawn from traditions that have crossed oceans and mountain ranges, these songs travel the same routes as the communities who carried them: from medieval Iberia across the Mediterranean, from the Caspian shores to the Aegean, from the mountains of Kurdistan to distant rivers. Four original compositions intertwine with traditional songs and works by other composers, creating a journey shaped by movement, exile, and belonging.
Thursday, April 16, 2026 | 5:30-7:00 pm
In person at the Magnes Collection of Jewish Art and Life, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA
Ticket prices:

FULL PRICE: $30.00 (Use promo code EarlyB5 for $5 off)
FRIENDS OF THE MAGNES: $20.00 (Look for the promo code in your inbox. For questions, email magnes@berkeley.edu)
STUDENTS: $5.00 (Current student ID will be required for entry)
MUSEUMS FOR ALL: $5.00 (EBT/SNAP card required for entry)

Buy Tickets

If you have any questions about accessibility or require accommodations to participate in this event, please contact us at magnes@berkeley.edu or call us at (510) 643-2526 with as much advance notice as possible.
Presented in partnership with the Center for Jewish Studies and the Center for Middle Eastern Studies at UC Berkeley.
Elana Sasson is a vocalist, composer, and bandleader creating music at the intersection of Kurdish and Persian traditions, contemporary jazz, and Mediterranean folkloric influences. She explores themes of identity and duality: weaving emotive modal melodies, intricate harmonic textures, and diverse instrumentation to dialogue between tradition and modernity.
Shaped by years of training with master musicians from Iran and Kurdistan and studies at the Berklee College of Music in Valencia, Elana’s clear voice reflects a fusion of rich tradition and personal expression. Her music has been celebrated with nods from global press such as Songlines Magazine, who described her voice as “an arrestingly beautiful focus” and gave a 4 star review to the album ‘Golestan’. Her new album, ’In Between’ released with PKMusik in April 2025 and received shining reviews from press, including a 4 star review from Songlines.
She has graced global stages at prestigious venues such as Fundación Juan March (Madrid, Spain), Palau de Les Arts Reina Sofia (Valencia, Spain), Stone Nest (London, UK), Yerba Buena Center for the Arts (San Francisco, California), and Fabrica Research Centre (Treviso, Italy). Elana has been featured in festivals across Europe such as UPV Jazz Festival in Valencia, Spain, Isole Che Parlano in Sardigna, Italy, and AtoJazz Festival in Sofia, Bulgaria.
Elana’s voice can be heard on Growing Songs, a 2024 album produced by esteemed Iranian vocalist Mahsa Vahdat, and her music was featured in the 2023 Venice Biennale-premiered film Tatami. She has collaborated with renowned musicians such as Matthieu Saglio, Mahsa Vahdat, Perico Sambeat, and Hadar Noiberg.
Elana holds a Master of Music in Contemporary Performance and Production from Berklee College of Music in Valencia and a BA in Design Media Arts from UCLA. She was a resident at Fabrica Research Centre in 2019 and is a member of the Iranian Female Composers Association (IFCA). elanasasson.com
Queralt Giralt Soler is a cellist, vocalist, composer, and music educator, recognized for her innovative fusion of cello and voice. With a style that blurs the boundaries between her influences from classical, jazz, folk, and contemporary music, she brings a deeply personal sound to every performance.
A graduate of ESMUC (Escola Superior de Música de Catalunya) in Barcelona, she completed her final degree project, “Aprenentatge musical i expressivitat artística,” with the distinction of summa cum laude, reflecting her strong commitment to excellence in both artistic and pedagogical fields. She later continued her studies at Berklee College of Music in Boston, where she received the prestigious Berklee World Tour Scholarship and the Berklee String Department Award 2025, in recognition of her outstanding artistic trajectory and contribution to the string community.
At Berklee, she was part of the Institute of Jazz and Gender Justice, founded by Terri Lyne Carrington, and received mentorship from pioneering artists such as Linda May Han Oh, Kris Davis, Eugene Friesen, and Jeremy Harman, among others, helping redefine the future of jazz and modern strings through equity, innovation, and experimentation.
Her artistic voice shines both in her solo work and in collective projects such as the Clarity String Quartet and the Zambra Collective, where she combines improvisation, storytelling, and diverse multicultural influences. She is also actively involved in interdisciplinary collaborations, including the DMI Fresh Media gallery installation “Echoes of Green Street,” for which she collaborated with visual artists and builders, composing and producing the music for the installation.
Based between the United States and Catalonia, she performs regularly on both continents. She has collaborated with a wide range of renowned artists, including Kenny Barron, Jacques Morelenbaum, Joe Lovano, Darol Anger, and the Latin Grammy-winning group Las Migas. Her performances have been heard on stages such as Carnegie Hall, the Detroit Jazz Festival, and numerous festivals and concert halls across Europe and the United States.
As an educator, Queralt is committed to fostering artistic expression, musical fluency, and creative confidence in students of all ages. Since 2024, she has been part of the teaching team at Tallers Musicals d’Avinyó, where she shares her experience in an environment that values exploration, collaboration, and artistic identity.
Bahar Badiei is a Brooklyn-based oudist, composer, and educator originally from Tehran, Iran, whose work spans Iranian classical music, contemporary concert music, jazz, and free improvisation. A graduate of Berklee College of Music on a Presidential Scholarship, her diverse background shapes a distinctive approach to composition and improvisation.
She has taught workshops on Iranian classical music at institutions including Silkroad and won first place at the Iranian Youth National Music Festival for two consecutive years. Bahar has performed at prestigious venues such as the Chicago Symphony Orchestra and National Sawdust, as well as festivals in Iran and the U.S., including the New York Oud Festival.
A member of Danilo Perez’s Global Jazz Messengers, she has collaborated with artists including John Patitucci, Brian Blade, Danilo Perez, and Bruno Raberg. She recently completed her master’s degree at the Berklee Global Jazz Institute.
All About Jazz has described her as “a master improviser,” whose playing is “free of tonal constraint.”
About the exhibition
Exploring the theme of water through the holdings of the Magnes Collection, Flowing through Time and Tradition traces how water flows through and shapes Jewish lives: enacting belief, sustaining life and communities, providing the means for spiritual cleansing, and mapping identities.
This program is generously supported by Denah Bookstein.

title: The Mischlinge Exposé
location: The Osher Marin JCC Plaza J, 200 North San Pedro Rd., San Rafael, 94903, United States
description: Carolyn Enger’s The Mischlinge Exposé weaves a multimedia tapestry around a little-known aspect of the Holocaust: the Mischlinge (a derogatory Nazi term for those neither fully Jewish nor fully Aryan). Interweaving video and audio testimony from Carolyn’s godmother and her father (both labeled Mischling, Grade A by the Nazis), with the music of composers from the salon period who converted to Christianity in the decades before the war, and works reacting to questions of identity after the war, the program vividly illustrates what it was like to be between worlds in Germany in the first half of the 20th century. Through the telling of her family story, Ms. Enger’s live documentary The Mischlinge Exposé directly addresses universally significant issues of identity and inclusion, encouraging empathy, tolerance, and engagement.

title: The Mischlinge Exposé: A music and multimedia project by Carolyn Enger

title: Gallery Tour | Flowing through Time and Tradition
location: Magnes Collection of Jewish Life and Art, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA, 94720, United States
description:
Stop by the Magnes for a staff-led gallery tour of the new exhibition, Flowing through Time and Tradition.s
Exploring the theme of water through the holdings of the Magnes Collection, Flowing through Time and Tradition traces how water flows through and shapes Jewish lives: enacting belief, sustaining life and communities, providing the means for spiritual cleansing, and mapping identities.
Sunday, April 19, 2026 | 1:00 – 1:30 pm
In person at the Magnes Collection of Jewish Art and Life, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA
RSVP

Please join us for Women, Water, and Care, our April 19 Beit Midrash Study Session directly following the Gallery Tour from 1:30-3:00pm. Please RSVP for the study session here.
The Magnes’s programs and exhibitions are supported by our community. Click here to make a suggested donation of $5 per tour.
If you have any questions about accessibility or require accommodations to participate in this event, please contact us at magnesprograms@berkeley.edu or call us at (510) 643-2526 with as much advance notice as possible.
Also on view: An Archive of Archives: Roman Vishniac’s Exhibition History | New York, 1971-72 and Time Capsules: Exploring the Permanent Collection.
Image at top: Jonah and the Whale in Haifa Port by Eugene Abeshaus (1939-2008), Tel Aviv, Israel, 1978. Gift of the artist, 94.26.3

title: Beit Midrash Study Session | Women, Water, and Care
location: Magnes Collection of Jewish Life and Art, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA, 94720, United States
description:
A FLOWING THROUGH TIME AND TRADITION PROGRAM
Readings and Discussion with Author Elissa Strauss
Join us for Women, Water, and Care, the seventh in a series of Beit Midrash Study Sessions inspired by the art and objects on view in Flowing through Time and Tradition.
Sunday, April 19, 2026 / 22 Nissan 5786
1:30 – 3:00 pm
In person at the Magnes Collection of Jewish Art and Life, 2121 Allston Way, Berkeley, CA
RSVP

Water has always been a medium for domestic work and caregiving. Washing bodies, preparing food, and cleaning require procuring and using water. In this Beit Midrash we will examine two stories of biblical matriarchs, Rebecca and Rachel, focusing on their relationship with water. We will also incorporate readings on care ethics and care economics to consider the true value of the care provided with and through water.
Please join us for a special gallery tour focusing on objects connected to the monthly Beit Midrash theme before each study session from 1:00-1:30pm. Please RSVP for the gallery tour here.
The Magnes’s programs and exhibitions are supported by our community. Click here to make a suggested donation of $10 per session or $55 for the full series.
If you have any questions about accessibility or require accommodations to participate in this event, please contact us at dalter@berkeley.edu or call us at (510) 643-2526 with as much advance notice as possible.
Program is subject to change.

About the guest teacher
Elissa Strauss is a journalist, essayist and cultural critic who has been writing about the politics and culture of parenting and caregiving for more than fifteen years. Her work appears in publications like the Atlantic, the New York Times, Glamour, ELLE, and elsewhere, and she was a former contributing writer at CNN.com and Slate. Her book, “When You Care: The Unexpected Magic of Caring for Others,” is out now from Gallery Books.
In addition to writing, she is a curator, speaker and artistic director of LABA: A Laboratory for Jewish Culture.
Photo credit: Laura Turbow Photography
About the series
Beit Midrash, meaning a house of study in Hebrew, is a series of eight creative, collaborative study sessions inspired by the art and objects on view in Flowing through Time and Tradition. Guided by the curator and guest teachers, we will engage with texts and artistic expressions to delve deeper into the exhibition objects and themes, to draw knowledge, surface reflections, and pour forth new perspectives.
About the exhibition
Exploring the theme of water through the holdings of the Magnes Collection, Flowing through Time and Tradition traces how water flows through and shapes Jewish lives: enacting belief, sustaining life and communities, providing the means for spiritual cleansing, and mapping identities.

Image at top: Tancrède Dumas (1830–1905) Egypt – Fellah Women Carrying Water [reproduction], Cairo, Egypt, 1860–1889. Glass plate slide. Gift of M. Rosengarten, 79.41.15

title: Game On! Mexican Train, Rummikub & Scrabble
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join others for a delightful afternoon of fun and games – literally! Choose from one of three games. Learn and play!

Mexican Train has become a popular game with dominos in which the goal is for participants to play all of the tiles from their hands onto one or more chains – or trains – emanating from a central hub or “station.”

Rummikub is a tile-based game for 2 or more players combining elements of the card game rummy and mahjong.

Scrabble is a popular word game for 2-4 players where participants use letter tiles to create words on a board, aiming to connect them to existing words (similar to a crossword puzzle).

Players of all levels are welcome!

Space is limited. Advance registration required.

title: Yom HaZikaron 2026: Remembering Those We Lost
description:
Park Heights (location provided upon registration)

Join Baltimore’s Shinshinim and Shlichim
Honor the memory of Israelis who were killed in acts of terror or who fell in battle through hearing their stories from the perspective of those who knew them.
RegisterEvent Details
Join Pearlstone, The Associated, Jewish Educational Services, and The Baltimore Jewish Council for an evening of commemoration and storytelling for Yom HaZikaron. Honor the memory of Israelis who were killed in acts of terror or who fell in battle through hearing their stories from the perspective of those who knew them. Then mark with us the transition from Yom HaZikaron to Yom HaAtzmaut, as we remember that our celebration of Israel’s independence is inseparable from the sacrifices that made it possible.
Questions? Contact Kate McDonald (kmcdonald@baltjc.org).

title: TBA Book Club: Titan of Tehran by Shahrzad Elghanayan
location: RSVP to Judyglick@gmail.com for Zoom link
description:
We will meet on Wednesday, April 22, viaZoom, to discuss the nonfiction book, Titan of Tehran: FromJewish Ghetto to Corporate Colossus to Firing Squad – My Grandfather’s Life, by ShahrzadElghanayan. Thebook is 287 pages, and was published in 2021. There are three copies availablethrough Link+: https://csul.iii.com/search?/XTitan+of+Tehran&SORT=DZ/XTitan+of+Tehran&SORT=DZ&extended=0&SUBKEY=Titan+of+Tehran/1,4,4,B/detlframeset&FF=XTitan+of+Tehran&1,1,;two of those three copies are housed in the San Francisco libraries, in caseyou have a membership there. The book is in audio format, available throughHoopla. The book is for purchase, and it there is a Kindle edition. Hopefully,we will stagger our reading, so that everyone who wants to take take it out ofthe library will be able to do so.

FromAmazon:
“Titanof Tehran,” a richly reported and elegantly rendered story, presents acompelling central character, historic sweep and moments that read likechapters in a thriller. The titan is Habib Elghanian – a self-madeindustrialist and the foremost Jew of his time in Iran, whom the Islamictheocracy targeted as the first civilian executed during the 1979 revolution.With Iran continuing to generate front-page news, his previously untold storyis painfully relevant, shedding light on that country’s persistent economic,political and social problems. “Titan of Tehran” brings that great, ancient,now-compromised nation into fresh and accessible focus.
The odysseyof Elghanian’s life and death is told by his granddaughter in an understatedstyle that nonetheless makes clear her powerful stake and personal place in theunfolding drama. Exploring universal themes of loss and longing, belonging and identity,she reconstructs and chronicles his ascent from Tehran’s Jewish quarter – “theedge of the pit” – to his business success that was instrumental in modernizingthe country to fatefully facing a firing squad.
“Titan ofTehran” serves as a monument to a man who might have disappeared in the mistsof history, even though his execution was reported worldwide on newspaper frontpages and in broadcast news reports. In his homeland, Elghanian wasmisrepresented, mistreated, maligned and murdered – but now won’t be forgotten.He is a riveting character whom readers will keep in their hearts – a titan,yes, but deeply, sadly, delightfully human, too.
Below are linksto three reviews of the book:
https://www.jewishbookcouncil.org/book/titan-of-tehran-from-jewish-ghetto-to-corporate-colossus-to-firing-squad-my-grandfathers-life
https://apnews.com/article/entertainment-middle-east-iran-arts-and-entertainment-tehran-af7e6501c3ae96c62e3bbd7a5004da75
https://haam.org/book-review-the-titan-of-tehran-by-shahrzad-elghanyan/
If you want evenmore information, there are several videos on YouTube regarding the book, aswell.

title: Jewish Green Business Network SF Summit
location: Monument, 140 9th St, San Francisco, California, 94103, United States
description:
Join Adamah’s Jewish Green Business Network at Climate Week SF for a high-level summit dedicated to the intersection of Jewish identity and climate leadership.
Attendees will engage in discussions on current changes in business practices, explore North American-Israeli green business collaborations, and build intergenerational connections that empower the next generation of climate leaders.
Why Attend?
– Curated Networking: Connect in person with a supportive community that are ready to help one another succeed in a common mission.
– Strategic Collaboration: Learn about business opportunities and emerging technologies that accelerate the transition to a green economy.
– Values-Driven Leadership: In a time of funding cutbacks, learn from inspiring speakers who align professional expertise with Jewish responsibility and global impact.
Whether you are looking to scale your impact, find strategic collaborators, or anchor your professional journey in shared values, this summit is the space to expand your network in a meaningful way.

title: ¡Cantamos!
location: JCC East Bay, 1414 Walnut Street, Berkeley, CA, 94709, United States
description:
It’s Time for ¡Cantamos!
Experimente la fusión rítmica de las tradiciones judía y latina con Verónica Freidkes, nuestra creativa música, educadora infantil y cantante argentina. Juntos celebraremos la primavera, sus flores y colores, y nuestra responsabilidad de cuidar a nuestro planeta, “Shomrei Adamah.”
Diseñado especialmente para familias con niños de 0 a 8 años. ¡Todos son bienvenidos!
¡Cantamos! es una colaboración entre JCC East Bay, Olamim , Jewtina y co , and Tkiya.
¡REGÍSTRESE HOY!

***

Experience the rhythmic fusion of Jewish and Latin traditions with Verónica Freidkes, our creative musician, child educator, and Argentinian singer. This session we’ll celebrate the many colors and blooms of springtime through multilingual songs and stories exploring the natural world and our commitment to “Shomrei Adamah” – protecting our planet.
Tailored especially for families with children ages 0-8. All are welcome!
¡Cantamos! is presented through a partnership among JCC East Bay, Olamim , Jewtina y co , and Tkiya.
REGISTER TODAY!

title: Israel in Focus with Jerusalem Post’s, Herb Keinon
description:
Bay Area Jewish Singles Havurah
Get the perspective beyond the headlines. Join us for a special event with the Jerusalem Post’s Herb Keinon, as part of the Jewish Federations of North America “Israel in Focus” national tour.

Through expert analysis and timely updates, Keinon will lead a deep dive into the Israel–Hamas conflict and the evolving challenges facing the region. This is a unique opportunity to move beyond the news cycle, engage in meaningful dialogue, and strengthen our collective understanding. Registration required [HERE](https://host.nxt.blackbaud.com/registration-form/?formId=d026ea05-8467-4c22-a9bb-65dcb5f8e351&envId=p-gQxjDCa1DkiFLH21jxgFhg&zone=usa)

We can go out for dinner after the event for those who are interested.

title: Game On! Mexican Train, Rummikub & Scrabble
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join others for a delightful afternoon of fun and games – literally! Choose from one of three games. Learn and play!

Mexican Train has become a popular game with dominos in which the goal is for participants to play all of the tiles from their hands onto one or more chains – or trains – emanating from a central hub or “station.”

Rummikub is a tile-based game for 2 or more players combining elements of the card game rummy and mahjong.

Scrabble is a popular word game for 2-4 players where participants use letter tiles to create words on a board, aiming to connect them to existing words (similar to a crossword puzzle).

Players of all levels are welcome!

Space is limited. Advance registration required.

title: Creating Hope: Pathways to Strength
location: The JCCSF, 3200 California St, San Francisco
description:
The annual gala celebrating the 34th anniversary of Shalom Bayit, the Bay Area’s center for domestic violence prevention and response within the Jewish community. Includes a reception, strolling dinner, and speaker program.
This year’s event will shine a light on financial abuse and how Shalom Bayit helps survivors achieve economic independence and safety.

Chaired by Sandi Bragar & Jerome Rossen

title: Game On! Mexican Train, Rummikub & Scrabble
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join others for a delightful afternoon of fun and games – literally! Choose from one of three games. Learn and play!

Mexican Train has become a popular game with dominos in which the goal is for participants to play all of the tiles from their hands onto one or more chains – or trains – emanating from a central hub or “station.”

Rummikub is a tile-based game for 2 or more players combining elements of the card game rummy and mahjong.

Scrabble is a popular word game for 2-4 players where participants use letter tiles to create words on a board, aiming to connect them to existing words (similar to a crossword puzzle).

Players of all levels are welcome!

Space is limited. Advance registration required.

title: Midweek Musical Matinee: "Classical Guitar thru the Ages"
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Come join us for a live, in-person concert filled with beautiful music and interesting insights.

Fan-favorite guitarist Marc Teicholz returns to play a concert of gorgeous works by master composers through the ages, including Fernando Sor (1778-1839), Manuel Ponce (1882-1948), Joaquin Turina (1882-1949), and Sérgio Assad (1952-).

title: Sivan Rosh Chodesh Circle
description:
Urban Adamah offers an ever-expanding calendar of community programs that integrate mind, heart, and body. Join us for a Shabbat celebration or other Jewish events and holidays, a volunteer work party, a gardening workshop, a family farm day, and so much more. We hope to see you on the farm soon!

Looking for ways to bring the farm home? Looking for new ways to celebrate an upcoming holiday? Or curious about past programs offerings? Check out our virtual resource guide for various at-home gardening tips, nature based projects for the entire family, and programs that create spiritual connection, and so much more!

title: Game On! Mexican Train, Rummikub & Scrabble
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Join others for a delightful afternoon of fun and games – literally! Choose from one of three games. Learn and play!

Mexican Train has become a popular game with dominos in which the goal is for participants to play all of the tiles from their hands onto one or more chains – or trains – emanating from a central hub or “station.”

Rummikub is a tile-based game for 2 or more players combining elements of the card game rummy and mahjong.

Scrabble is a popular word game for 2-4 players where participants use letter tiles to create words on a board, aiming to connect them to existing words (similar to a crossword puzzle).

Players of all levels are welcome!

Space is limited. Advance registration required.

title: Intro to Judaism Unit 3
description:
Judaism is built around community, ritual, tradition, and prayer. If you are Jewish, interested in converting or raising a Jewish family and looking to learn more, delve deeper, and connect — learn with us!
Our engaging, university-accredited Intro To Judaism course is interactive and encourages questions, discussion, and hands-on learning. No knowledge of Hebrew is required. This Introduction to Judaism class will be taught in three units. For those who are on the path for conversion, please register for the full course — all three units, as this is not a drop-in model.
Click here for schedule and registration!
Looking to join our next cohort in the fall? Contact Hilary at helfman@bethsholomsf.org for more information

title: Virtual Minyan: Tuesday Morning
description:
Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, and Sunday minyan takes place on Zoom (Note: 8 a.m. on Sundays).

Join your community in morning prayer any day of the week!

Please note that on secular, national holidays daily minyan will start at 8:30 a.m.

On Tuesdays, stay to delve into the weekly parsha with Torah study (begins at 8:15).

Join Zoom Meeting: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/81632373785

Meeting ID: 816 3237 3785

title: Morning Minyan Hybrid
location: In person & over Zoom
description: A brief lay led morning service. An opportunity to say kaddish and mi shebeirach.

title: Tuesday Morning Torah Study
description:
Join us on Zoom every Tuesday morning at 8:15 a.m. to look at the weekly parsha and delve into the teachings that our text has brought us throughout the past millennia, and to today.

Weekly discussions will be led by CBS Congregant, Marilyn Heiss, with occasional teachings from the Rabbis. You're also welcome to sign-in early and catch some morning minyan!

Join Zoom Meeting: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/81632373785

Meeting ID: 816 3237 3785

Please note, Tuesday morning minyan and Tuesday morning Torah study share the same Zoom link.

title: Lunchtime Learning Circle Hybrid
location: Fireside Room and Zoom
description: Rabbi Lisa Kingston leads study of the Pirkei Avot. Bring your sack lunch. All are welcome. Hebrew knowledge is not necessary.

title: Tuesday Evening Minyan
description:
Tuesday evening minyan will take place in the Gronowski Family Chapel.
This is an in-person only minyan. To attend online, please join us any morning of the week, except on Shabbat.
We welcome anyone from the greater Bay Area community to attend.
Please check the calendar for changes or cancellations due to secular holidays or otherwise.

title: Virtual Minyan: Wednesday Morning
description:
Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, and Sunday minyan takes place on Zoom (Note: 8 a.m. on Sundays).

Join your community in morning prayer any day of the week!

Please note that on secular, national holidays daily minyan will start at 8:30 a.m.

Join Zoom Meeting: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/81632373785

Meeting ID: 816 3237 3785

title: Midweek Musical Matinee: "Modern Masterpieces"
location: Osher Marin JCC, 200 North San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA, 94903, United States
description:
Come join us for a live, in-person concert filled with beautiful music and interesting insights.

Join pianist Keisuke Nakagoshi and violinist Patrick Galvin for a beautiful concert featuring music by three modern masters — Claude Debussy, Sergei Prokofiev, and Amy Beach — and a program that includes:

C. Debussy – Sonata in g minor for Violin and Piano, L. 140
S. Prokofiev – Sonata No. 1 in f minor for Violin and Piano, Op. 80
A. Beach – Romance, Op. 23

title: Mah Jongg
description: Meet in person to play Mah Jongg at CBS in the Main Meeting Room.

title: Baby, Me and Community
description:
Join music maestro Jonathan Bayer and members of the Beth Sholom staff for song, prayer, dance, and community building.

Ages newborn to 2 (and those who care for them) are invited to make connections to Jewish melodies, rhythms and joy, create lifelong friendships, share the ritual of Shabbat, and bond with the ones you love.

Members: FREE
Nonmember: $18 per session

Please register in advance for Baby, Me and Community.

title: SING Kabbalat Shabbat Service
description:
Join us monthly for SING, a lively musical Kabbalat Shabbat led by Rabbi Amanda Russell and musicians. Come early to connect and enjoy light refreshments, then welcome Shabbat with song and spirit.

SING is a joyful way to transition from the week into Shabbat, with music, community, and a warm, inclusive space for all.5:30 p.m. - Nosh and schmooze

6 p.m. - Service

WHERE: CBS Chapel

title: Baby, Me and Community
description:
Join music maestro Jonathan Bayer and members of the Beth Sholom staff for song, prayer, dance, and community building.

Ages newborn to 2 (and those who care for them) are invited to make connections to Jewish melodies, rhythms and joy, create lifelong friendships, share the ritual of Shabbat, and bond with the ones you love.

Members: FREE
Nonmember: $18 per session

Please register in advance for Baby, Me and Community.

title: Shabbat Friday Evening Service
description:
Join us every Friday evening in the Chapel for Kabbalat Shabbat—a weekly opportunity to come together in community and welcome Shabbat.
Some weeks include musical instruments, while others are lifted by the ruach (spirit) of voices alone. However we gather, it’s a time to slow down, reflect, and ease into the peace of Shabbat.
WHERE: CBS Chapel

title: Erev Shabbat Service
location: In-person or join over Zoom
description: Welcome in Shabbat with joyous singing and meaningful prayer.

title: Torah Study - Hybrid
location: Chapel and Zoom
description: Led by our clergy, explore your own religious ideas in an active dialogue with other congregants and through the wisdom of traditional commentaries. This ongoing weekly study session is open to all.

title: Shabbat Services (In-Person)
description:
Join Rabbi Russell this Saturday for in-person Shabbat services.

Engage in prayer, song, Torah reading, and a delicious Kiddush. Enjoy community as you share authentic and meaningful religious and spiritual experiences.

WHERE: CBS Sanctuary

title: Shabbat Yeladim: Just for Kids!
description:
Shabbat Yeladim: Just for Kids! Bring your kids to childcare while you enjoy Shabbat elsewhere in the building. Come together for Adon Olam and our community Kiddush.
Serves children newborn to five years old.
This experience is free for members and $18/ child for guests.
Date: Saturdays
Time: 9:30 a.m. - 12 p.m.
Place: Beth Sholom Family Preschool’s Gan Adamah Classroom
Community guests, please register for Shabbat Yeladim.

title: Camp Aleph | Summer 2026
location: Chabad Jewish Center of Petaluma, 3921 Fabian Way, Petaluma, CA, 94303
description:
Aleph is for Awesome. Aleph is for Amazing. Aleph is for #1.
We are delighted that you are joining us, and we are certain that your children will have a wonderful camp experience. The counselors and staff at Camp Aleph are eager to greet your children with many fun and exciting programs planned for this year.

Camp Aleph offers a 2-week summer program for children ages 4 through 13.
Camp Aleph for girls ages 13-14.
Camp Aleph Adventures program for boys ages 11-13.

We are looking forward to an amazing summer!

Join Rabbi Russell this Saturday for in-person Shabbat services.

Engage in prayer, song, Torah reading, and a delicious Kiddush. Enjoy community as you share authentic and meaningful religious and spiritual experiences.

WHERE: CBS Sanctuary

title: Shabbat Yeladim: Just for Kids!
description:
Shabbat Yeladim: Just for Kids! Bring your kids to childcare while you enjoy Shabbat elsewhere in the building. Come together for Adon Olam and our community Kiddush.
Serves children newborn to five years old.
This experience is free for members and $18/ child for guests.
Date: Saturdays
Time: 9:30 a.m. - 12 p.m.
Place: Beth Sholom Family Preschool’s Gan Adamah Classroom
Community guests, please register for Shabbat Yeladim.
Register for Shabbat Yeladim

description:

title: Morning Minyan Hybrid
location: In person & over Zoom
description: A brief lay led morning service. An opportunity to say kaddish and mi shebeirach.

title: Tuesday Morning Torah Study
description:
Join us on Zoom every Tuesday morning at 8:15 a.m. to look at the weekly parsha and delve into the teachings that our text has brought us throughout the past millennia, and to today.

Weekly discussions will be led by CBS Congregant, Marilyn Heiss, with occasional teachings from the Rabbis. You're also welcome to sign-in early and catch some morning minyan!

Join Zoom Meeting: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/81632373785

Meeting ID: 816 3237 3785

Please note, Tuesday morning minyan and Tuesday morning Torah study share the same Zoom link.

title: Lunchtime Learning Circle Hybrid
location: Fireside Room and Zoom
description: Rabbi Lisa Kingston leads study of the Pirkei Avot. Bring your sack lunch. All are welcome. Hebrew knowledge is not necessary.

title: Tuesday Evening Minyan
description:
Tuesday evening minyan will take place in the Gronowski Family Chapel.
This is an in-person only minyan. To attend online, please join us any morning of the week, except on Shabbat.
We welcome anyone from the greater Bay Area community to attend.
Please check the calendar for changes or cancellations due to secular holidays or otherwise.

title: Morning Minyan
location: Chapel, 327 MacArthur Blvd, Oakland, CA, 94610, United States
description:
Our morning minyan meets every Monday and Thursday in the chapel and lasts approximately one hour. We use the older Conservative siddur (prayer book) compiled by Rabbi Morris Silverman for daily prayer.
The service is always followed by a light breakfast in the social hall, lovingly prepared by minyan regulars.

title: Baby, Me and Community
description:
Join music maestro Jonathan Bayer and members of the Beth Sholom staff for song, prayer, dance, and community building.

Ages newborn to 2 (and those who care for them) are invited to make connections to Jewish melodies, rhythms and joy, create lifelong friendships, share the ritual of Shabbat, and bond with the ones you love.

Members: FREE
Nonmember: $18 per session

Please register in advance for Baby, Me and Community.

title: SING Kabbalat Shabbat Service
description:
Join us monthly for SING, a lively musical Kabbalat Shabbat led by Rabbi Amanda Russell and musicians. Come early to connect and enjoy light refreshments, then welcome Shabbat with song and spirit.

SING is a joyful way to transition from the week into Shabbat, with music, community, and a warm, inclusive space for all.5:30 p.m. - Nosh and schmooze

6 p.m. - Service

WHERE: CBS Chapel

title: Erev Shabbat Service
location: In-person or join over Zoom
description: Welcome in Shabbat with joyous singing and meaningful prayer.

title: Torah Study - Hybrid
location: Chapel and Zoom
description: Led by our clergy, explore your own religious ideas in an active dialogue with other congregants and through the wisdom of traditional commentaries. This ongoing weekly study session is open to all.

title: Shabbat Friday Evening Service
description:
Join us every Friday evening in the Chapel for Kabbalat Shabbat—a weekly opportunity to come together in community and welcome Shabbat.
